::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# This document contains code for Lab 2

# Make a temperature variable
temp_C <- 11

# Math to transform from Celsius to Fahrenheit
(temp_C * (9/5)) + 32
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 51.8
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Temperatures for the entire week
temperatures <- c(12, 
                  13, 
                  16, 
                  14, 
                  17, 
                  16, 
                  18)

# Days of the week
days <- c("Sunday", 
          "Monday", 
          "Tuesday", 
          "Wednesday", 
          "Thursday", 
          "Friday", 
          "Saturday")

# Make a dataframe of temperatures and their corresponding days
days_temps <- data.frame(days = days, 
                         temps = temperatures)

# Convert temps to Fahrenheit
days_temps$temps <- (days_temps$temps * (9/5)) + 32

# Days with swimable temperatures
sum(days_temps$temps >= 60)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 4
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
## Movie Ratings

# Ratings for my top 5 movies
top5_ratings <- c(86, 
                  44, 
                  94, 
                  70, 
                  91)

# My top 5 movies
top5_movies <- c("Never Been Kissed",
                 "Fire Island",
                 "Singing in the Rain", 
                 "In the Mood for Love", 
                 "Sound of Music")

# Min rating
min(top5_ratings)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 44
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Max rating
max(top5_ratings)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 94
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Mean rating
mean(top5_ratings)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 77
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Stats professor movies
stats_prof_movies <- c("10 Things I Hate About You", 
                       "The Notebook", 
                       "Legally Blonde", 
                       "Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind", 
                       "Mean Girls")

# Check if my movies are included in the stats professor movies
top5_movies %in% stats_prof_movies
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
## These logical values tell me if each of my top movies (from the top5_movies vector)
## is included in the vector stats_prof_movies
## Since they are FALSE, we don't seem to like the same movies!


## In this script, I've created 7 objects: 
## temp_C, temperatures, days, days_temps, top5_ratings, stats_prof_movies, and top5_movies
```
:::
